title: pittosporum undulatum subsp. undulatum
intro: Pittosporum undulatum subsp. undulatum, also known as the Tasmanian hops or mock orange, is a plant species native to eastern Australia and Tasmania, characterized by its fragrant flowers and distinctive, undulating leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Pittosporum undulatum subsp. undulatum include its potential to reduce stress, improve sleep, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and tinctures, are used to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
